page: Amazon Web Services g6e.xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs, consisting of 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, running at a base speed of 3.4 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. For accelerated workloads, it includes a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 45 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided via a 250 GB NVMe SSD, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The server exhibits strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, and average single-core scores in Geekbench. However, multi-core CPU performance, multi-threaded decompression, and memory bandwidth fall into the bottom 25%. Conversely, the integrated NVIDIA L40S GPU delivers top-tier performance in the top 10% for large language model inference, achieving 6130.77 tokens per second for prompt processing and 134.56 tokens per second for text generation on a medium 7B model.

Given its hardware profile, the g6e.xlarge offers a specialized resource balance, combining a high-capacity GPU with a modest 4-vCPU allocation. This makes it highly cost-efficient for GPU-bound tasks that do not require massive parallel CPU processing, such as small-to-medium LLM inference, graphics rendering, and machine learning model evaluation. However, it is less suitable for CPU-heavy multi-threaded applications, high-throughput database operations, or intensive web serving, where its multi-core and memory bandwidth limitations present clear performance tradeoffs.
description: Amazon Web Services g6e.xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance designed for GPU-accelerated workloads. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a 250 GB local NVMe SSD. The hardware is anchored by a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 45 GB of VRAM. While the instance exhibits weak multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth in benchmarks, its GPU capabilities deliver top-tier results for large language model inference, processing over 6130 tokens per second on a 7B model. This hardware profile makes the g6e.xlarge highly efficient for graphics rendering, machine learning inference, and GPU-bound workloads that do not require extensive multi-threaded CPU resources.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, a 250 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 45 GB VRAM for high-performance AI inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU optimized for AI inference workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-focused instance pairing dedicated AMD EPYC processing with an NVIDIA L40S GPU for efficient machine learning inference and graphics workloads.
bullet_points: NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 45 GB VRAM for accelerated computing
4 dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 vCPUs running at 3.4 GHz
32.0 GB DDR4 system memory and 250 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in LLM inference and prompt processing benchmarks
Optimized for graphics rendering, machine learning, and GPU-bound tasks
